text: ‘Tested by discipline’; some of the Greek manuscripts have ‘chastised’, or perhaps simply ‘schooled’; others, more plausibly, a ‘travelled man’.

vv. 10, 11: The Latin here seems to have suffered from much confusion. ‘Mischief’ and ‘knavery’ in the Greek are ‘discernment’ and ‘resourcefulness’; ‘if he is led astray’ is ‘when he has travelled’. The Latin translator seems to have given two separate renderings of the same verse, neither of them accurate.
In the Greek, ‘I know more than I am ready to tell’.
It is difficult to see the appositeness of this phrase. The rendering ‘The Lord is only (approachable) for those who wait upon him in loyal duty’ does not represent the Latin.
